name: Father's Day
description: Origins, traditions, and trivia about the holiday celebrating dads.

questions.q: Who is generally credited with founding Father's Day in the United States?

source.notes: Inspired by her widowed father raising six children alone.

questions.q: In what year was the first Father's Day held in the U.S.?
Which U.S. state first held a statewide Father's Day celebration?

source.notes: Sonora Smart Dodd lived in Spokane, WA.

questions.q: Which U.S. president signed Father's Day into law as a permanent national holiday in 1972?
On which Sunday is Father's Day observed in the United States?
Which Catholic feast day is celebrated as Father's Day in many traditional Catholic countries (e.g. Spain, Italy, Portugal)?

source.notes: St. Joseph is the patron of fathers and workers.

questions.q: Which Catholic religious order is most credited with promoting St. Joseph's Day as a fatherhood feast since the late Middle Ages?

source.notes: Franciscan Order has long championed devotion to St. Joseph.

questions.q: Which 1907 mining disaster in Monongah, West Virginia inspired one of the earliest Father's Day services in the U.S.?
choices: A coal mine explosion that killed hundreds of fathers
A flood at a hydropower dam

source.notes: Grace Golden Clayton organized a 1908 service in Fairmont, WV; over 360 men died, ~250 of them fathers.

questions.q: Where is the apostrophe correctly placed in 'Father's Day' (per the convention chosen by Sonora Smart Dodd)?

source.notes: To honor each family's own father — same convention Anna Jarvis used for Mother's Day.

questions.q: Which globally-observed November 19 holiday focuses on men and boys?
On what date is Father's Day celebrated in many Catholic-majority countries (e.g. Spain, Italy)?

source.notes: Tied to St. Joseph's feast day.

questions.q: When does Australia and New Zealand celebrate Father's Day?

source.notes: Both countries celebrate on the first Sunday of September.

questions.q: Which gift is the most popular U.S. Father's Day purchase, according to NRF surveys?

source.notes: Greeting cards top the list — Father's Day is the 4th-largest U.S. card-sending occasion.

questions.q: Which color rose did Sonora Smart Dodd suggest wearing on Father's Day to honor a living father?

source.notes: Red rose for living fathers; white rose for those who have passed.

questions.q: Which 'Father of his Country' is the U.S.'s most famous archetypal father?

source.notes: Despite the title, Washington had no biological children — he was stepfather to Martha's children.

questions.q: Which Greek god, depicted with a thunderbolt, is the 'father of gods and men'?
Which Coptic Christian observance celebrates fathers on July 20?

source.notes: Tied to St. Joseph in the Coptic tradition.

questions.q: Which 1972 Mac Davis country-pop song became a Father's Day classic?

source.notes: 'Cat's in the Cradle' was actually written by Harry Chapin in 1974 — a famously bittersweet father-son anthem.

questions.q: What food gift is traditionally given to fathers in Germany on 'Vatertag'?

source.notes: Germany's Vatertag is on Ascension Day; tradition is a 'Herrenpartie' — men hike or wagon-tour while drinking beer.

questions.q: What is the literal English translation of the Spanish phrase used for Father's Day, 'Día del Padre'?

source.notes: Celebrated March 19 (St. Joseph's Day) in Spain.
